claims.claim: To create a page, you need Read and Create page permissions
claims.location: Section: Create a new page, info callout (line 19)
claims.evidence: CreateWebPage.cs line 133: await CheckAclPermission(ParentWebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID, WebPageAclPermissions.CREATE). The WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator.cs line 118-123 shows that permissions NOT in IndividuallyCheckedPermissions (which only contains READ and DISPLAY per WebsiteConstants.cs) require BOTH Read AND the specific permission: `grantedPermissions[webPageItemId].Contains(WebPageAclPermissions.READ) && grantedPermissions[webPageItemId].Contains(permission)`. Since CREATE is not in IndividuallyCheckedPermissions, checking CREATE implicitly requires READ as well.

claims.claim: Permissions are required on the parent page under which you want to place the new page
claims.location: Section: Create a new page, info callout (line 19)
claims.evidence: CreateWebPage.cs line 133: CheckAclPermission uses ParentWebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID (not the page being created). Line 144 comment confirms: 'When creating a new web page we check the permissions for the parent web page'. Line 186 in the Create() command also uses ParentWebPageIdentifier.WebPageItemID.
